messages.content:
You are a question answering assistant. Your job is to generate a SPARQL query to answer a given user question.

You should follow a step-by-step approach to generate the SPARQL query:
1. Determine possible entities and properties implied by the user question.
2. Search for the entities and properties in the knowledge graphs. Where applicable, constrain the searches with already identified entities and properties.
3. Gradually build up the SPARQL query using the identified entities and properties. Start with simple queries and add more complexity as needed. Execute intermediate queries to get feedback and to verify your assumptions. You may need to refine or rethink your current plan based on the query results and go back to step 2 if needed, possibly multiple times.
4. Use the answer or cancel function to finalize your answer and stop the generation process.

Available knowledge graphs:
- "wikidata" at https://qlever.dev/api/wikidata
  - "entity" index (type="fuzzy"): Entities indexed by their labels and synonyms
  - "property" index (type="embedding", modalities="text"): Properties indexed by their labels, synonyms, and identifiers

Index types used:
- "embedding": Retrieves items by cosine similarity between their embeddings and the query embedding. The embedding model used depends on the index and may support text, images, or both.
- "fuzzy": Retrieves items by overlap between their label words and the query keywords. The query keywords must not match label words exactly, but some fuzziness is allowed. The longer a query keyword is, the more it can deviate from a label word and still be considered a match, though it will also contribute less to the overall score. No special query operators like AND/OR are supported.

SPARQL prefixes for use in function calls:
- bd: http://www.bigdata.com/rdf#
- cc: http://creativecommons.org/ns#
- data: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Special:EntityData/
- dc: http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/
- dct: http://purl.org/dc/terms/
- foaf: http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/
- geo: http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#
- geof: http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/
- gn: http://www.geonames.org/ontology#
- hint: http://www.bigdata.com/queryHints#
- imdb: https://www.imdb.com/
- math: http://www.w3.org/2005/xpath-functions/math#
- ontolex: http://www.w3.org/ns/lemon/ontolex#
- owl: http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#
- p: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/
- pq: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/
- pqn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value-normalized/
- pqv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value/
- pr: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/
- prn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value-normalized/
- prov: http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#
- prv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value/
- ps: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/
- psn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value-normalized/
- psv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value/
- qb: http://purl.org/linked-data/cube#
- qfn: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/function#
- ql: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/builtin-functions/
- qlss: https://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/spatialSearch/
- rdf: http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#
- rdfs: http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#
- ref: http://www.wikidata.org/reference/
- s: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/statement/
- schema: http://schema.org/
- skos: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#
- v: http://www.wikidata.org/value/
- void: http://rdfs.org/ns/void#
- wd: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/
- wdno: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/novalue/
- wdt: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/
- wdtn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct-normalized/
- wikibase: http://wikiba.se/ontology#
- xsd: http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#

Additional rules to follow:
- Explain your thought process before and after each step and function call.
- Do not ask the user for clarification until you have made an initial attempt at completing the task. When the task input is incomplete or ambiguous, proceed based on reasonable assumptions.
- Do not use "SERVICE wikibase:label { bd:serviceParam wikibase:language ..." in SPARQL queries. It is unsupported by the used QLever SPARQL endpoints. Use rdfs:label or similar properties to get labels instead.
- Always execute your final SPARQL query before giving an answer to make sure it returns the expected results.
- The SPARQL query should always return the actual identifiers / IRIs of the items in its result. It additionally may return labels or other human-readable information, but they are optional and should be put within optional clauses unless explicitly requested by the user.
- Do not stop early if there are still obvious improvements to be made to the SPARQL query. For example, keep refining your SPARQL query if its result contains irrelevant items or is missing items you expected.
- Do not perform additional computation (e.g. filtering, sorting, calculations) on the result of the SPARQL query to determine the answer. All computation should be done solely within SPARQL.
- For questions with a "True" or "False" answer the SPARQL query should be an ASK query.
What British Honour is held by the only scorer against Fulham away from home in a 1-1 draw during the 1983–84 Liverpool F.C . season in League Cup

tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. sport (wd:P641 as wdt/p, matched via "sport played"), also known as "play", "plays", "sport played", "sports":
  - sport that the subject participates or participated in or is associated with
2. season of club or team (wd:P5138 as wdt/p, matched via "season of club"), also known as "season of athletics program", "season of club", "season of team", "sports season of club or team":
  - club or team that played the season
3. country (wd:P17 as wdt/p, matched via "P17"), also known as "host country", "land", "sovereign state", "state":
  - sovereign state that this item is in (not to be used for human beings)
4. instance of (wd:P31 as wdt/p, matched via "P31"), also known as "example of", "has type", "is a", "is an", "is of type", etc.:
  - type to which this subject corresponds/belongs. Different ... is an instance of mountain; volcano is a subclass of mountain
5. time period (wd:P2348 as wdt/p, matched via "era"), also known as "epoch", "era", "historical period", "historic era", "historic period", etc.:
  - time period (historic period or era, sports season, theatre ... in which the subject occurred or with which it is associated
6. followed by (wd:P156 as wdt/p, matched via "then"), also known as "comes before", "continued as", "next", "next element", "next is", etc.:
  - immediately following item in a series of which the subject ... replaced, e.g. political offices, use "replaced by" (P1366)]
7. follows (wd:P155 as wdt/p, matched via "is after"), also known as "before was", "comes after", "is after", "preceded by", "predecessor", etc.:
  - immediately prior item in a series of which the subject is a ... item, e.g. political offices, use "replaces" (P1365)]

content.message: I need to find information about matches played by Liverpool against Fulham during the 1983–84 season. Let me search for properties related to matches or games that could help identify the specific match:

tool_calls.name: search_property
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. number of wins (wd:P1355 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "matches won"), also known as "games won", "matches won", "match victories", "sporting wins", "victories", etc.:
  - number of sporting matches, games or events won
2. league or competition (wd:P118 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "competition"), also known as "competition", "cup", "division", "sports league", "tournament":
  - league or competition in which team or player has played, or in which an event occurs
3. number of losses (wd:P1356 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "matches lost"), also known as "defeats", "games lost", "L", "losses", "lost", etc.:
  - number of sporting matches, games or events lost
4. member of sports team (wd:P54 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "team"), also known as "club played for", "member of team", "of team", "part of team", "played for", etc.:
  - sports teams or clubs that the subject represents or represented
5. winner (wd:P1346 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "1st place medalist", "awardee", "champ", "champion", "has winner", etc.:
  - winner of a competition or similar event, not to be used from ... qualified with "for work" (P1686)) nor for wars or battles
6. participant (wd:P710 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "between"), also known as "accused", "agent", "attendee", "belligerents", "between", etc.:
  - person, group of people or organization (object) that ... has role" (P3831). Use P1923 for participants that are teams.
7. terminus location (wd:P609 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "between"), also known as "between", "departure point", "destination", "from", "to":
  - location of the terminus of a linear feature
8. member of (wd:P463 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "club"), also known as "band member of", "club", "in musical group", "is member of", "member of musical group", etc.:
  - organization, club or musical group to which the subject ... position, such as a member of parliament (use P39 for that)
9. in opposition to (wd:P5004 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "against"), also known as "against", "opposed", "opposed to", "opposes", "opposition to", etc.:
  - object of a social action
10. competition won (wd:P2522 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "won"), also known as "contest won", "event won", "winner of", "won", "won event":
  - competition or sports event won by the subject

I need to search for properties of the match that could indicate it was a 1-1 draw. Let me search for match properties related to scores:

tool_calls.name: search_property
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. number of points/goals/set scored (wd:P1351 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "score for"), also known as "GF", "goals for", "goals scored", "number of goals", "number of points", etc.:
  - goals / points scored in a match or an event used as qualifier to the participant. Use P1358 for league points.
2. instrumentation (wd:P870 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "scoring"), also known as "arranged for", "instruments", "instruments called for", "instruments employed", "instruments used", etc.:
  - combination of musical instruments employed in a composition or accompanying a (folk) dance
3. orchestration (wd:P11862 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "scoring"), also known as "instrumentation", "instrumentation in notated form", "instrumentation notation", "notated instrumentation", "notated orchestration", etc.:
  - an enumeration of the instruments used in a musical composition or arrangement described using any system of score notation
4. test score (wd:P5022 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "assessment score":
  - score that the item achieved in a test; use as qualifier for "test taken" (P5021)
5. commemorates (wd:P547 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "marks"), also known as "anniversary of", "birthday of", "celebrates", "commemorandum", "honors", etc.:
  - what or whom is commemorated by the place, monument, memorial, or holiday
6. ranking (wd:P1352 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "peak position", "place", "placing", "position", "rank":
  - subject's ordinal position as qualitatively evaluated relative to other members of a group
7. score method (wd:P1443 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "score system"), also known as "score system", "score type", "scoring method", "scoring metric", "scoring system":
  - qualifier of "points/goal scored by (P1363)"
8. rating (wd:P4271 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv):
  - qualifier to indicate a score given by the referenced source indicating the quality or completeness of the statement
9. school class (wd:P7174 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "grade"), also known as "class", "cohort", "grade", "school cohort", "stage":
  - (qualifier) class of a given year/time period to which the person belonged in an educational institution
10. points/goal scored by (wd:P1363 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "scorer"), also known as "goal scored by", "points scored by", "scored by", "scorer":
  - person who scored a point or goal in a game
